extern type Number;
cap Add { fn add(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_add(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
impl Number: Add { fn add(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_add(a, b)) }

#[inline(always)]
fn double(x: Number): Number = Add.add(x, x)
fn main(): Number = double(10)

===EXPECT===
# `#[inline(always)]` happy path: `double` is a small fn with a resolvable
# Perform (default `impl Number: Add`). The attribute forces inline-at-
# call-site regardless of caller count / body size heuristics.
#
# Invariants:
#   - `double` fn definition is absent from the JS (no `function double(`).
#   - `double__lto` clone is absent (inline only, no clone).
#   - `double`'s body is inlined into `__main_cps`: `const x = 10;` plus
#     `js_add(x, x)` expression.
#   - LTO validation would have errored if `double` had an unresolvable cap;
#     here it resolves via the typeclass default, so compile succeeds.
__main_cps
js_add(10, 10)
!function double(
!double__lto
!__lto_x
!__caps.Add_Number.add(__caps, 10
